Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase; metaphorical compound noun + verb construction
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To act proudly despite having little substance or merit
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in agrarian metaphors contrasting fertile land and barren pride in Dima Hasao villages
First Example Sentence: Nangha mwjang jwla gwsu, baiha khamja nangha da ase.
Second Example Sentence: Baidi mwjang jwla gwsu, khana khara dang.
Third Example Sentence: Anga mwjang jwla gwsu nisinai, bima nangha dangja.

Second Example Sentence: Baidi dima habasi, jwla gajao nai.
Third Example Sentence: Nangha dima habasi nai, mwjang ase.

Second Example Sentence: Anga khamja thangja nisinai, habadang ase.

Third Example Sentence: Nangha jonthai jwla dang nisinai, mwjang gajao.

Third Example Sentence: Anga habai jwla saijao nisinai, jwla dangja.
